A-hole management. Okay, so what is A-hole management? Believe it or not, there will be assholes who enter your gym. I know. Crazy stuff. So A-hole my numbers. Let's objectify these A-holes. So if you have 50 members right now, for example, you probably have two or three people that are just crazy people. You probably know who they are. They're kind of everybody's paying the ass. The thing is, is if all of a sudden you sign up 150 people, you're going to have 10 crazy people in your hands plus the two or three other ones. And so all of a sudden you feel like you got all sorts of crazy, right? And that is because we disproportionately weigh client negatives in our minds. And so in our minds, we think, you know, oh my gosh, if we have 10 people that are having a terrible time, everyone's having a terrible time, but the reality is that those 10 people are the only ones. And a lot of times you should just give them their money back and just move on with your life because they will take so much more time and attention from you. And I know it is really hard to do this. And most of you still won't listen to this because you're afraid of giving someone their money back. But I am telling you, like you should be more afraid of them staying, right? And continuing to like to track from your trainers and make them hate their lives and have people who don't want to see that woman in class. And I can't tell you the amount of times where I had people who literally after I fired someone were like, oh, thank God, I was going to have to quit soon because I was so tired of hearing this girl complain, right? So like you lose good people when you keep bad people. So don't do that. All right. Fire the crazies. I think I made that point. So when someone is having any issues when it comes to like weight loss, right, you need to be empathetic for how they're feeling but firm with the results, meaning I can't tell you the amount of times most people are just not going to hear it. Like I cannot tell you. So the first time around this way, way, way, way, way, way, way back, very first time, I think I had like, it's really bad. I had like a 20% success rate. It was horrible. And so I called my dad up and I was like, Oh my gosh, I think they're thyroid plummeted. I think I, you know, I over cut them on the diet, but I was giving all these reasons, right? And he's a doctor. Not that that gives him knowledge about nutrition because it doesn't, but he does know about compliance. And he was like, Alex, he's like, they didn't follow it. And I was like, no, no, that's not it. You clearly don't understand. He was like, they didn't follow the plan. And this was my first time doing it. So I wasn't really checking in on that part. I didn't have them like taking pictures. I didn't like, I wasn't even following up with them keeping the weight. I was really just trying to like keep my gym running, right? And so eventually I did learn because I just became really, really hard about results. And like, and I just want you to know that like, you need to have absolute conviction in the process. And I know it's really hard because you have not done it yet. But like, this program works because there is no secret to it. We're getting, we're putting people in a significant deficit, giving someone a huge emotional win early on, so that they can get enough progress, get enough momentum that they'll be able to propel themselves to long term weight loss. Believe it or not, if you look at research studies, when it comes to long term weight loss, usually having periods of fast weight loss in the beginning creates more psychological buy-in, which creates longer term success. What? Yes, we are not fitness competitors, or they are not fitness competitors. Like you have probably already cut and died at a million times and already know how your body works. And you do it slowly to will it yourself down. But general population needs a big emotional win in order to crush it. So you need to be empathetic with how they're feeling, but firm that they're probably not following it, like for real. Why? Because even fitness professionals cheat. All right? So GenPop, come on. All right? So I'm being like very, very real with you. Like people lie. Like you really need to like confront that. A lot of people stressy. They're ashamed of it. They don't want to admit it. People, a lot of their doctors about whether they drink and they smoke. Like why don't you think they'll lie to you? All right? For real. Okay. Customer service 101. So the angry boat. This is something that I feel very passionate about. If you want to find out more, you can go to just go to the Facebook group and search how to handle angry customers. That's in the free group, high ticket, high volume on Facebook. But customer service 101 is that and I got some Richard words. There can only be one person in the anger. But so if you ever have someone who's upset about something, so if it's something about results and whatnot, you have to address that from a nutrition standpoint. And that's fine. But if someone's being a crazy person about the bathroom wasn't clean, right? Some or like the trainer was too minutes late, which is definitely a problem. Right? It's an issue. Like they are 100% correct. Right? But you might feel that their response is disproportionate. Right? They might be disproportionately upset for the reality. Right? And mind you, they're not entrepreneurs. They don't have as thick a skin as you. And so like in their, in their delicate lives, something big happened. So the premise of the anger boat is this, there can only be one person in the angry boat. And your job is to be angry than them about the thing of their anger about. Okay? So let me give you an exam. Trainer is five minutes late. And they're like, the trainer is five minutes late today, blah, blah, blah, you know, complain, complain, complain. You're like, you've got to be five minutes. Are you serious? Like you were waiting there. How many people were there? Oh my gosh, this is ridiculous. Thank you for telling me this is absurd. I'm going to put him immediately on suspension. Like I might have to fire him. And then all of a sudden, you'll see they start backpeaseling. And they're like, you know what? It actually wasn't that big of a deal. It was only five minutes. You know, John's a good guy. I'm sure he just got caught in traffic. Sometimes it happens, right? And then they become the reasonable one. Why? Because there can only be one person in the angry boat. So the thing is, is that if you try and do the same thing, like, listen, John's a good guy, you probably got really busy, you know, there's traffic, you probably over subbed his phone died. And that's why he's alarmed and go off whatever it is, right? Whatever it is, they will then feel like you're not listening to them. And then they'll get angrier because they're trying to get a response from you. So you're like, they get angrier, then you try and dull them down more than they get angry. So instead, what you need to do is you need to take the high ground and get angry than them. And then they all of a sudden start to escalate. So that is customer service 101. If there's ever any issues, you need to be more upset about it than they are. I'm telling you, this is going to save you so much time, so much like effort, like do that. And that takes us to the end of a home management.
